title: Woman on the Twenty Act of 2025
short_title: Woman on the Twenty Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill says that all twenty dollar bills made after December 31, 2028 must have a picture of Harriet Tubman on the front. Harriet Tubman was a brave woman who helped enslaved people escape to freedom. The Treasury Secretary, who is in charge of making money, has to show everyone what the new twenty dollar bill will look like by December 31, 2026. This means people will see the design two years before the new bills start being printed.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Woman on the Twenty Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. In eighteen seventy five, Congress made the dollar the official money unit of the United States. In eighteen seventy seven, the Bureau of Engraving and Printing, which is part of the Treasury Department, started printing all United States paper money. The Federal Reserve Act of nineteen thirteen created the Federal Reserve as the country's main bank and set up a national banking system. The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System began issuing new paper money called Federal Reserve notes. The Secretary of the Treasury has traditionally chosen the designs on Federal Reserve notes with help from the Bureau of Engraving and Printing. The United States Federal Reserve notes that are made today have these portraits. President George Washington is on the one dollar bill. President Thomas Jefferson is on the two dollar bill. President Abraham Lincoln is on the five dollar bill. Alexander Hamilton is on the ten dollar bill. President Andrew Jackson is on the twenty dollar bill. President Ulysses S. Grant is on the fifty dollar bill. Benjamin Franklin is on the one hundred dollar bill. There are also several types of Federal Reserve notes that are no longer made. These include the five hundred dollar bill with President William McKinley, the one thousand dollar bill with President Grover Cleveland, the five thousand dollar bill with President James Madison, the ten thousand dollar bill with Salmon P. Chase, and the one hundred thousand dollar bill with President Woodrow Wilson. Since paper money was first widely used, no woman has ever had the honor of being featured on paper money. In June twenty fifteen, Treasury Secretary Jack Lew announced that a woman's portrait would be featured on the new ten dollar bill. After a long time of public discussion between the public and the Treasury Department, on April twentieth twenty sixteen, Treasury Secretary Lew announced that for the first time in more than a century, the front of our money will feature a woman's portrait. Harriet Tubman would be on the twenty dollar note. The Secretary also announced the back of the new ten dollar note would show an image of the historic march for women's right to vote that ended at the steps of the United States Treasury Department. This would feature the leaders of the suffrage movement including Lucretia Mott, Sojourner Truth, Susan B. Anthony, Elizabeth Cady Stanton, and Alice Paul. Finally, the Secretary announced the back of the five dollar note would show an image of historic figures at the Lincoln Memorial who shaped our history and democracy, including Marian Anderson, Eleanor Roosevelt, and Martin Luther King Junior.

sections.title: Section 3 Harriet Tubman on the Twenty Dollar Note
sections.text: The law is changed to add new requirements. Starting after December thirty first twenty twenty eight, no twenty dollar bill can be printed unless it clearly shows a portrait of Harriet Tubman on the front of the bill. The Secretary of the Treasury must show the public what the new twenty dollar bill with Harriet Tubman's portrait will look like no later than December thirty first twenty twenty six.
